text: In one sentence, what's your content about and who is it for?
subtext: e.g., '15-minute weeknight dinners for parents of picky eaters.'
why: A specific topic and audience lets us judge how crowded the space is and who you're actually competing with.

text: How will people get your teaching or content?
options: Live sessions I deliver (one-on-one or small group)
A mix of content and live sessions
why: How you deliver this changes your whole cost and revenue model — published content, courses and live sessions all work differently.

option_notes.Published content (videos, podcast, news: We'll focus on audience growth, ad/sponsorship rates and content-based monetisation for your report.
option_notes.Online course or paid community: We'll look at course pricing benchmarks, platform costs and what it takes to launch and fill a cohort.
option_notes.Live sessions I deliver (one-on-one or s: We'll focus on session pricing, your time capacity and how you'll book and retain clients.
option_notes.A mix of content and live sessions: We'll cover both audience-building and session delivery, since you're combining the two.

text: What's your primary way of making money from it?
why: Your main way of making money shapes what revenue benchmarks and platform costs we research for you.

option_notes.Ads: We'll look at ad rates for your niche and the audience size you'd realistically need to earn from them.
option_notes.Sponsorships: We'll research typical sponsorship rates for your audience size and how brands usually find creators like you.
option_notes.Paid subscription: We'll cover subscription pricing benchmarks, churn and what it takes to keep paying members.
option_notes.Course sales: We'll look at course pricing, launch strategies and what converts an audience into paying students.
option_notes.Coaching / consulting: We'll focus on hourly or package pricing and how you'll find and convert one-on-one clients.
option_notes.Affiliate: We'll look at typical affiliate commission rates and how much traffic or audience you need to make it worthwhile.
option_notes.Merch: We'll cover production costs, minimum order quantities and the margins on physical merchandise.

why: Knowing if you're stacking revenue streams changes how we model your overall income potential.

option_notes.None: We'll model your numbers around a single revenue stream, keeping projections simple and realistic.
option_notes.Ads: We'll add ad revenue as a smaller secondary line alongside your main income source.
option_notes.Sponsorships: We'll factor in sponsorship income as a secondary stream on top of your primary model.
option_notes.Paid subscription: We'll factor in subscription income as an added stream on top of your primary model.
option_notes.Course sales: We'll factor in course revenue as a secondary stream alongside your main income source.
option_notes.Coaching / consulting: We'll factor in coaching income as a secondary stream alongside your main income source.
option_notes.Affiliate: We'll factor in affiliate income as a smaller secondary stream on top of your main model.
option_notes.Merch: We'll factor in merch revenue as a secondary stream, alongside production costs and margins.

text: How much have you published already?
why: How much you've already published tells us whether to focus on starting from zero or growing an existing audience.

in: A mix of content and live sessions

text: How big is your current audience?
why: Audience size is one of the biggest levers on how fast you could monetise — it changes our growth timeline assumptions.

in: A mix of content and live sessions

text: How will you run your sessions?
why: Whether sessions happen online or in person changes your costs, your reach and how many clients you can realistically serve.

option_notes.Online (video calls): We'll skip travel and venue costs and focus on the tools and platforms you'll need to run sessions.
option_notes.In person: We'll factor in travel, venue or space costs and the geographic limits on how many clients you can serve.
option_notes.Both online and in person: We'll cover both the tools you need online and the travel/venue costs for in-person sessions.

in: Live sessions I deliver (one-on-one or small group)
A mix of content and live sessions

text: How will you charge for sessions?
why: How you price sessions changes your cash flow and how we model your revenue over time.

option_notes.Per single session: We'll model revenue session-by-session and look at typical one-off pricing in your field.
option_notes.Session packages: We'll look at package pricing and how bundling affects your cash flow and client commitment.
option_notes.Monthly subscription/retainer: We'll model steady recurring revenue and typical retainer pricing for your field.
option_notes.Not sure yet: We'll show you a couple of common pricing approaches so you can decide once you've seen the numbers.

in: Live sessions I deliver (one-on-one or small group)
A mix of content and live sessions

text: How much time will you put into this each week?
why: How much time you can put in each week shapes how realistic our growth and income timeline is.
